All States · Indiana

Indiana Dietitian License

Dietitians Certification Board. $20 application. 2 to 4 Weeks processing.

Last verified 2026-02-25 · Board-sourced data
↗ Official Board Portal (opens in a new tab)

Quick Facts at a Glance

Application Fee
$20
$20 application fee (fingerprint fees additional)
Processing
2 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
Renewal
$20
Every 2 years (by Dec 31, even years) + 30 CE hours
License Duration
2 Years (expires Dec 31, even years)
From date of issuance
Licensing Authority

Dietitians Certification Board

Indiana Professional Licensing Agency (IPLA)
Phone
(317) 234-2060
Email
pla3@pla.in.gov
Mailing Address
402 West Washington Street, Room W072
Indianapolis, IN 46204
Physical Address
402 West Washington Street, Room W072
Indianapolis, IN 46204
Credential Categories

Indiana Dietitian License Types

Certified Dietitian (CD)

Indiana's state certification for dietitians. While optional for use of the 'Registered Dietitian' title, most employers require both the national RD credential and the state CD certification. Allows use of the 'Certified Dietitian' title.

Application Process

How to Get Licensed in Indiana

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a bachelor's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program in dietetics, human nutrition, or a related field. Complete 900–1,000 hours of supervised dietetics practice through an accredited internship.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians.

Submit the Online Application

Apply online through the IPLA licensing portal. Pay the $20 non-refundable application fee. Include two passport-style photographs and a copy of your current CDR registration card.

Complete Fingerprint Background Check

Submit to a mandatory fingerprint-based criminal background check. The fingerprinting fee is paid separately to the designated vendor.

Required Documents

Application Checklist

  • ✓
    Official College Transcripts Sent directly from the university, showing degree in an approved field and date awarded.
  • ✓
    CDR Registration Card Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration credential.
  • ✓
    Two Passport-Style Photographs Recent photographs meeting standard passport specifications.
  • ✓
    Fingerprint Background Check Mandatory fingerprint-based criminal background screening.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules
Indiana does not offer a provisional or temporary dietitian certification. Applicants must hold active CDR registration and complete all requirements before certification is issued.
Reciprocity & Endorsement
Indiana does not have a formal endorsement or reciprocity pathway. Out-of-state dietitians apply through the standard application process. Holding current CDR registration satisfies the education and examination requirements. You must still submit the application, $20 fee, transcripts, and complete the Indiana fingerprint background check.
Travel Dietitian Timeline
If your assignment start date is within 5 weeks, apply immediately and call the board at (317) 234-2060 to confirm current processing times.

Note: Indiana does not offer a standard provisional license, so your license must be issued before your first day.
Maintenance

Renewal Requirements

Indiana dietitian licenses renew every 2 years (by dec 31, even years) + 30 ce hours. Renewal fee is $20.

Frequently Asked Questions

Indiana Dietitian FAQ

How much does an Indiana dietitian certification cost?

The initial application fee is only $20 — one of the lowest in the country. The biennial renewal is also $20 (plus a $10 late fee if submitted after December 31). Fingerprint background check fees are paid separately to the vendor.

Is Indiana dietitian certification required to practice?

Indiana's Certified Dietitian (CD) credential is technically optional for practicing as a Registered Dietitian (RD). However, most employers in Indiana require both the national RD credential and the state CD certification.

What continuing education is required in Indiana?

Indiana requires 30 hours of continuing education during each two-year licensure period for active renewal. Continuing education is not required for inactive renewal.
